title: Admin identity determined by online list order: implicit load-bearing invariant
description: What is awkward: The 'admin' role is determined by who is first in the online Vec<String> (commands.rs:150, 202, 218, 241). This works because: (1) relay sends DirectoryList ordered by first_seen ASC from the users table, (2) UserJoined appends to the end, (3) UserLeft calls retain(). The oldest-first_seen user is always admin. But this triple dependency between relay SQL order, client list maintenance, and the admin rule is not documented anywhere -- it's fully implicit. A relay change that alters DirectoryList ordering (e.g., for performance) or a client bug in list maintenance silently changes who is admin. The consequence is not a crash -- it's two clients both thinking they're admin, both calling add_member and remove_member, producing MLS epoch conflicts. Suggested rewrite: At minimum, add a comment in commands.rs explaining the invariant and its relay dependency. Better: relay should explicitly designate the admin in DirectoryList or AuthOk.
